plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-402 RP13.P04.M06.S04-RP13.P05.M04.S05 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-402 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP13.P04 phase (M06 Synthetic Fixture Set tail, M07 Test And Golden Case Set, M08 Hermes Evidence Packet, M09 Claude Review Packet, M10 Closeout And Next Handoff) and opens the RP13.P05 foundation (M00 Scope Inventory, M01 Contract Draft, M02 Type And Shape Definition, M03 Primary Implementation Slice, M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head) as ten descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H13), Claude review packet (C13), and CP00-403 handoff, without opening payments, payment matching, AR aging, or journal entry runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-402 is a clean descriptor-only increment in the RP13 payments-core program (range RP13.P04.M06.S04-RP13.P05.M04.S05, 150 units, risk C), consistent with the established CP00-392..401 pattern. The pack-level posture is honest: manifest/command-evidence report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status implemented_pending_review; the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification flag (not an enterprise-trust or runtime claim) and is supported by coverage validation, dedicated tests, and the contract validator. Coverage ties out exactly: deliverable {ui37,claude_review13,security_audit12,implementation37,fixture35,test10,hermes_evidence6}, phase {P04:87,P05:63}, and all micro-phase/micro-title counts match the baseline, manifest, registry requirements, and the live closeout-pack-plan entry (CP00-402 is the current live pack; next is CP00-403). No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold across freezeCp402Result, the no-write attestation, and the committed contract block (writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, synthetic_only=true, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; every descriptor row descriptor_only=true/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe_errors_only=true/raw_payload_included=false). Authority boundary is intact: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Hermes is used only for the deterministic descriptor_only..._no_write review packet/receipt, not product runtime. CP178+ review-hardening guardrails are wired (forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing refs, and final-approval/enterprise-trust claims; hardened_review_sequence enforces baseline->readiness->raw-capture->normalize->validate->adjudicate->inspect->closeout->commit). This is a clean first review attempt: readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON, invalid_attempts_preserved=[], and no prior raw-output.json/review-receipt.json exist. changed-file-scope correctly isolates the eight pack implementation refs from unrelated dirty/untracked files (the .docx, workbook/*, and the ldip invalid-attempt JSON), which were not treated as pack evidence. The validator deep-equals the generated descriptor/requirements/attestation against the committed contract and reports the full validation battery (incl. npm test, build, git diff --check) at exit 0. Claude acted strictly as a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob only) and is not the final approver; human final approval remains required and the enterprise trust claim is closed outside this receipt. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-402 payments core p04 closeout p05 foundation descriptor pack
